CheckTitle: Ensure domains have a valid SSL certificate provisioned
Description: Checks whether Vercel domains have an SSL certificate provisioned. Vercel automatically provisions and renews SSL certificates for properly configured domains. A missing SSL certificate indicates a configuration issue that leaves traffic unencrypted.
Risk: Without an SSL certificate, traffic between users and the domain is transmitted in plain text. This exposes sensitive data such as authentication tokens, form submissions, and personal information to interception via man-in-the-middle attacks. Search engines also penalize non-HTTPS sites, reducing visibility.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to the Vercel dashboard
2. Navigate to the project Settings > Domains
3. Verify the domain's DNS records point to Vercel correctly
4. Vercel will automatically provision an SSL certificate once DNS is properly configured
5. If issues persist, remove and re-add the domain

Recommendation.Text: Ensure domain DNS records are properly configured to point to Vercel. Once DNS is validated, Vercel automatically provisions and renews SSL/TLS certificates. Check the domain configuration in the Vercel dashboard if the certificate is not being issued.
